title: UT Health San Antonio Readies $38M VITAL-H Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, Dapagliflozin to Slow Aging
description: A $38M federally-backed trial will enroll 700+ adults aged 60-65 to test whether rapamycin, semaglutide, and an SGLT2 inhibitor can slow biological aging markers and preserve intrinsic capacity.

title: ARDD 2026 Conference Relocates to Boston After Copenhagen Cancellation
description: The leading Aging Research and Drug Discovery conference has been unexpectedly moved from Copenhagen to Boston, sparking rapid reorganization across the longevity research community.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: Analysis of the longevity biotech market valued at $30.7B in 2026, projected to reach $52.8B by 2035, with epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and organ regeneration identified as key investment verticals.

title: ARPA-H Awards $144M for PROSPR Program to Build Aging Trial Infrastructure
description: Seven research teams receive up to $144M from ARPA-H to build phase 3 trial infrastructure for aging interventions, including rapamycin, metformin, and semaglutide in healthy adults.

title: Cambrian Bio Receives $30.8M ARPA-H Award for Next-Gen Rapamycin Analog Trials
description: Cambrian BioPharma secures $30.8M through ARPA-H PROSPR program for clinical trials of a next-generation rapamycin analog, while Linnaeus Therapeutics receives $22M for oncology-derived aging drugs.

title: WHOOP Enters $34.5M ARPA-H-Backed Bid to Quantify Aging
description: Wearable company WHOOP joins ARPA-H PROSPR program with $34.5M backing to develop aging quantification tools using continuous health monitoring data.

title: Rapamycin Deep Dive: Safety Confirmed but Longevity Evidence Gap Remains
source: Center for Food as Medicine & Longevity
description: Comprehensive review of PEARL trial, Kell/Oxford immune study, and Elicit/Consensus analyses concludes low-dose rapamycin appears safe for one year but no completed human trial has proven biological age slowing.

title: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by Four Months in COSMOS Trial
description: Analysis of 958 participants shows daily multivitamin slowed all five epigenetic clocks over two years, with greatest benefits for those biologically older at baseline.

title: Annovis CEO Eyes Spring Breakthrough for Alzheimer's Drug
description: Annovis Bio anticipates a pivotal clinical readout this spring for its Alzheimer's disease drug candidate, targeting neurodegeneration at the intersection of aging biology.

title: Juvenescence Advances Aging Drug to Phase 2 Trial
description: Juvenescence, a longevity drug development platform backed by $283M in funding, progresses its lead candidate into Phase 2 clinical testing.

title: Scientists Successfully Freeze and Rewarm Mouse Brain Slices
description: German researchers achieve first successful vitrification and rewarming of mouse brain tissue with neuronal function recovery, a milestone for cryopreservation science.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Comprehensive roundup featuring Altos Labs ($3B), Retro Biosciences ($1.2B), NewLimit, Cambrian Bio, and other top longevity startups with latest funding and pipeline details.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising 2026: $6.9B Concentrated in Top 5
description: Analysis of 90+ longevity startups shows Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, and Insilico Medicine capture roughly $6.9B; NOVOS receives strategic investment from Unilever Ventures.

title: David Sinclair at WGS 2026: Aging Could Be Reversible Within 10-20 Years
description: At the 2026 World Government Summit in Dubai, Harvard's David Sinclair reported 75% reversal of aging markers in animal tissues and said human clinical trials of epigenetic reprogramming are imminent.

title: Oxytocin + Alk5 Inhibitor Combo Increases Mouse Lifespan by 73%
description: Study in aged mice shows oxytocin plus TGF-beta pathway inhibitor improved muscle strength, memory, and blood proteins while extending remaining lifespan by 73% and median lifespan by 14%.

title: Puget Sound Startups Invest in the Science of Staying Healthy
description: Coverage of how high-profile tech billionaires like Jeff Bezos and Sam Altman are driving major VC funding into Pacific Northwest longevity and healthspan biotech ventures.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic and NK Cell Advances
description: Monthly roundup highlights Nature Aging paper on mitochondrial stress boosting senolytic effectiveness and Aging Cell study on restoring natural killer cell function in aging.

title: First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial Cleared by FDA
description: Life Biosciences receives IND clearance for ER-100, the first-ever human trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming, targeting optic neuropathies with patient enrollment beginning in March 2026.

title: Richter Sharpens Ovarian Aging Focus Following Celmatix Asset Deal
description: Mainstream pharma company Richter acquires Celmatix ovarian biology assets to expand therapies targeting ovarian aging, reinforcing women's health as a central longevity focus.

title: Wearable Startup Temple Secures $54M for Brain Monitoring
description: Temple raises $54M for a head-worn wearable that continuously monitors cerebral blood flow and brain activity, providing real-time cognitive health insights.

title: Boosting Longevity Research with Strategic Coordination: Norn Group Update
description: Norn Group reports $34M deployed through Impetus Grants for high-impact longevity research, plus community programs and a clinical trial framework for aging interventions.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Hit $45.8B by 2033
description: Market research projects longevity biotech growing at 20.4% CAGR from 2026 to 2033, driven by regulatory support, strategic collaborations, and heightened investment in therapeutic innovation.

title: Investors Eye Explosive Growth in Anti-Aging Therapies: Market Targets $420B Milestone
description: New market analysis projects the global anti-aging therapy market could reach $420B, driven by convergence of biotech innovation, AI drug discovery, and consumer healthspan demand.

title: Genes Influence Lifespan Far More Than Previously Thought: Heritability Above 50%
description: Weizmann Institute study in Science journal revises lifespan heritability estimates upward to 55%, more than doubling previous figures after accounting for extrinsic mortality factors.

title: Mimio Health Trial Shows Fasting-Mimetic Delivers Benefits Without Dieting
description: Clinical trial data shows Mimio Health's fasting-mimetic supplement delivers metabolic benefits of fasting without requiring actual dietary restriction.
